prose:
Fukui electrophilicity (f⁺) for formaldehyde — DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF RKS, no density-fitting), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1.
Geometry: fixed Cartesian coordinates from the provided file “/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz”; no re-optimization (single-point N, N±1 calculations). Thus the property is DFT//input-xyz.
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (engine subcommand “fukui”) calling PySCF 2.x.  Cation (N−1) and anion (N+1) states were computed at doublet multiplicity (M=2) as per default protocol.
Convergence: all three SCFs converged; integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true; step counts are in the live log.
Result: the most electrophilic atom is C (atom 2, index 1) with f⁺ = 0.7083913 (dimensionless).  The most nucleophilic site is O (atom 1) with f⁻ = 0.46742464; dual descriptor highlights C (0.596) as the preferred electrophilic site and H atoms as nucleophilic (−0.298).
Live log: the calculation streamed to a file of the form “fukui_<timestamp>.out” in the current working directory (announced at launch and available for inspection).  Plot of atomic Fukui indices saved to /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/argo_o3/20260706-183020_formaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_02_plot.png.
No additional warnings were reported beyond those echoed above; SCF and charge-conservation checks passed.

Method/provenance summary:
• Property: atomic Fukui functions f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, dual descriptor (dimensionless); headline value = 0.7083913 for atom C.
• Level of theory: B3LYP/def2-TZVP (RKS, exact 4-center integrals).
• Geometry: supplied .xyz (no relaxation) ⇒ single-point DFT on fixed geometry.
• Solvent: gas phase (no continuum model).
• Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral singlet); cation/anion states: +1/−1 charge, multiplicity 2.
• Backend: PySCF via chemkit fukui-reactivity skill.
• Converged SCFs; integrity.trustworthy = true.
• Warnings (verbatim) reproduced in this report.

Provenance: computed (no experimental data involved).
